name: Prunus Red Stinkwood Bark
description: An evergreen montane forest tree of sub-Saharan Africa. The bark contains high concentrations of phytosterols (beta-sitosterol) and pentacyclic triterpenes that inhibit inflammatory pathways in prostatic tissue, clinically validated for urinary distress and associated fevers.
preparation: Bark tea infusion: Steep 5g of dried shredded bark in 500ml of boiling water for 15 minutes. Strain and administer 150ml warm twice daily.
safetyAlert: Extremely safe in standard clinical doses. Prolonged use is well tolerated.
interactions: None known. Safe to use alongside standard clinical hydration.
sideEffects: Mild transient gastrointestinal irritation or constipation in highly sensitive patients.
contraindications: None reported in mature populations. Avoid combining with heavy direct hormone therapies unless clinically audited.
countyStorageAdvisory: Mombasa: Store bark slices in heavy-duty canvas bags placed inside dry wooden drawers to shield against ambient seaside humidity. Lodwar: Keep in closed metal storage bins to safeguard active phytosterol structures from extreme ambient heat and sand storms.
dosingRules: Children: Not indicated. Adults: Steep 5g of dried shredded bark in 500ml of boiling water for 15-20 minutes. Administer 100ml to 150ml of the strained warm infusion twice daily after meals.
